verdict: PASS — all hard checks clear; reading is within length budget (1390 words / 7 min per meta.json, corroborated by manual estimate), primary source [1] cited throughout, no forward references, no scope creep, no unsupported assertions, beginner formatting throughout.

0_format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is '# Foundation models — trained once at scale, usable for many tasks'. Meta sidecar parses and carries all required fields. Exactly six sections in correct order.

1_length.notes: Meta.json reports 1390 words / 7 min, both within target. Manual paragraph-by-paragraph estimate corroborates ~1370–1420 words. The task brief notes a prior measurement of 1700 words / 9 min — that measurement does not match the current artifact text; the current artifact passes. No hard fail triggered.

2_citation_discipline.notes: All three resources cited inline and present in bibliography. No orphans in either direction.

3_no_forward_references.notes: LLM used with definition on first appearance (line 21: 'LLM (Large Language Model)'). 'parameters' back-referenced with parenthetical gloss '(the learned numerical settings introduced in topic 3.5)' on line 18. Tokens, temperature, sampling, hallucination, evaluation not used. Fine-tuning and RAG are explicitly named-and-deferred ('covered in topic 4.2' / 'covered in topic 4.3') — not taught — conforming to the one-sentence pointer rule.

sampled_assertions.assertion: one large AI model trained on massive amounts of data, which can then be applied to many different tasks without being retrained from scratch
Foundation model — a large AI model trained on broad, diverse data at massive scale
Think of a building's foundation: it is not the finished structure, but it is the base
training a leading model can cost tens of millions of dollars
only a small number of organisations — large technology companies and well-funded research labs — train foundation models from scratch
a model trained to predict the next word turned out to be useful for dozens of completely different tasks
Krutrim (Ola) — India's first publicly announced foundation model
A foundation model's knowledge is frozen at the end of its training run
The model is not retrained from scratch for each new use

4_faithfulness.notes: Comparison table row wording ('Low training cost' vs corpus 'Cheap to train') is a light paraphrase, not a new factual claim. No assertions introduce facts absent from the corpus.

beginner_readability.notes: 'open-weights' (line 113) has an inline gloss. 'LLM' spelled out on first use. 'parameters' back-referenced. Building analogy precedes definition (concrete-first). No hedging phrases found. No assessment IDs or grade weights mentioned.

formatting.notes: No paragraph exceeds 6 sentences. Multi-item lists used for characteristics, capabilities, practical principles, worked example steps. Bold-term definitions applied to 'Foundation model', 'Multimodal model', 'Prompting', and section sub-concepts. Inline examples (cat/dog classifier, building analogy, three-org worked example) appear outside the Worked Example section.
